caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Supplied roles and labor share are 5411-parent data, not a measured 541120 payroll mix; lawyers are explicitly excluded from 541120 by S1.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions are task judgments, not observed headcount reductions or a claim that AI may execute regulated acts.
inputs.pi_dist: No 541120 pricing study exists; S4 is a small-law-firm proxy and S5 is one provider's price list.
inputs.pi_moat: This estimates operator retention of savings, not survival of paid demand; legal-document software and lawyer competition are not quantified.
inputs.t50_years: S4 does not measure 541120 and its survey population includes lawyers, who are excluded from this industry.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The proxy may either overstate or understate specialist notary-office use; it establishes no code-level census.
inputs.historical_analogs: No historical code-specific analogue was found; this is directional only.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of located evidence is not evidence of no succession issue.
inputs.active_consolidators: An undisclosed local roll-up could exist; no transaction database was available.
inputs.buy_mult: No reopened 541120 transaction comparable supports this value.
inputs.exit_mult: No reopened 541120 transaction comparable supports this value.
inputs.pricing_structure: S5 is one provider and cannot establish national pricing or price elasticity.

dataset_inputs.n_band: No official national establishment, revenue, margin or transaction series was found for 541120; the 2,000-office population and revenue/margin assumptions are judgmental upper-bound inputs.
dataset_inputs.n_band: S5 is a single provider price list, not representative revenue, utilization or EBITDA evidence.
dataset_inputs.n_band: A rare multi-office specialist could exceed the modeled range; candidate-level diligence must verify NAICS scope before treating it as a target.

cross_checks.terminal_value: S2 and S3 establish authorized acts and qualifications in two states but do not establish national exclusivity or durable paid demand for 541120; the DURABLE terminal class remains a low-confidence inference.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

a.rationale: The supplied 5411 role mix remains a low-quality parent proxy, but it is directionally closer after correcting the business to legal-document work. AI assists drafting and administration, while authorized approval, execution, archival and professional judgment remain human functions under the state models in S2 and S3.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 5411 ($158,392,472,386) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 5411 ($376,347,774,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: ancestor 5411. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact identity, model routing, schema, dataset injection, byte-identical finalization and memo rendering, and arithmetic all pass. Census supports the corrected narrow 541120 boundary, and the registered sources support their expressly limited legal-authority, adoption-proxy and price-anchor facts. The zero-target conclusion remains useful as a low-confidence feasibility screen, not a measured market statistic: its office-count, revenue, margin, ownership and multiple inputs are openly judgmental, while the labor and adoption evidence are broad disclosed proxies. The state statutes also do not independently prove durable national paid demand, so the terminal label requires the additional validator caveat above. None of these limitations can reverse the kill thesis driven by zero B and M under the selected estimates.

sources.title: U.S. Census Bureau 2022 NAICS definition: 541120 Offices of Notaries
sources.what_it_supports: The assigned industry's legal-document drafting, approval and execution scope; the exclusion of ordinary witnessing-only notary-public services to 541199.

sources.title: Louisiana Revised Statutes 35:2, General powers of notaries public
sources.what_it_supports: A code-relevant civil-law-notary example: authority over wills, conveyances, contracts and authentic acts.

sources.title: Florida Statutes, Chapter 118 Civil-Law Notaries
sources.what_it_supports: Civil-law-notary qualifications, appointment, authentic-act authority and archival obligations; a second code-relevant state model.

sources.title: American Bar Association, The Legal Industry Report 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Broad legal-services proxy evidence on uneven generative-AI use, small-firm legal-specific implementation, drafting correspondence and operations.

sources.title: Louisiana Notary Service Price Guide
sources.what_it_supports: One provider's posted $105/hour document-preparation price, used only as an illustrative service-price anchor and not as industry-average revenue evidence.

title: Offices of Notaries
